spec_match.rationale: Covers part of the spec (1 of 8 named API surfaces) — distinct angle worth picking up.

interactive.rationale: Real controls + script-driven side-effects make this hands-on, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: Page handles several edge cases (errors, fallbacks, feature detection, denials) — not just the happy path.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Has 4 raw hex colour value(s) inline — should use --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens instead.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for webnn
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: WebNN. navigator.ml entry point. MLContext via createContext({powerPreference, accelerated}). MLGraphBuilder builds DAG of MLOperand; build() compiles to MLGraph (async). Ops: conv2d, convTranspose2d, matmul, gemm, pooling (avg/max/l2), activations (relu, sigmoid, tanh, gelu, elu, softmax, hardSwish), normalization (batch/layer/instance), reduction (sum/mean/max), elementwise (add/sub/mul/div), te...

openQuestions.title: Tighten the design-system compliance
openQuestions.detail: Either replace raw hex with --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens, or pair every button background override with a color override (the global button rule sets both — overriding one alone risks light-on-light).

summary: Workable concept with a couple of gaps. 2 open question(s) queued.
